what: diagnosed pulse FLATLINE from spine tail-reads only; the 03:52Z PULSE note existed further up — the alarm I was building refuted its own author
guard: claims about records come from instruments — station spine N <match> / tally / log — never from tails or glances

what: claimed 01:15Z beat did wave-topup from an unfiltered spine tail; it ended IDLE — required a spine AMENDMENT
paid: 2026-07-04, wrong claim entered the spine, amendment + re-derivation (05:01Z)
guard: same guard; instrument beat my read twice in one night — the discipline does not hold as prose, only as the cheaper call

what: handoff live-edge claimed wave-2 NOT topped (T1-T3 3/8) from a stale PULSE note; ledger tally said T1 8/8 T2 6/8 — the stale claim crossed a molt
paid: 2026-07-04, next instance woke to a false live edge; caught only by re-running the tally
guard: any number entering a handoff/record is re-measured at write time, not quoted from a note

what: accused Specimen 0 of fabricating citation arXiv:2512.24601; the paper was real — the accusation was the fabrication
paid: 2026-07-02, false accusation entered CASE-RECORD.md; correction documented in 24601.md + Exhibit D
guard: plays/refute.md — one adversarial pass before any accusation or root-cause claim enters a record (the 24601 rule)

what: ledgered turn 13 as GAIN with measure landed-ok-first-try in the SAME call as the verify; the verify FAILED (BOM-mangled route refused). The seal must be a separate act AFTER observed verification
paid: 2026-07-04, false GAIN entry in spiral.jsonl requiring CORRECTION entry
guard: verify and seal never share a tool call; the ledger append happens only after reading the verify output

what: sealed spiral turns 22-24 with hand-typed FUTURE timestamps (07:12-07:36Z, real clock ~06:43-06:48Z); prior instance's turns 7-21 show the same disease (turn 21 t=07:01Z predates this session's 06:37Z wake). The witnessed ledger now notarizes fabricated times
paid: 2026-07-04, wrong-order timeline frozen into witnessed spiral.jsonl; corrections are appends, the wrong bytes stay
guard: ledger 't' fields come from a clock read at append time, never typed - structural fix: append through a stamping verb (station seal, built same session)

what: quoted 'certified lifetime=5' all session in vitals/wake/journal; the ledger held cache_eviction certified TWICE (fresh holdout seeds 101,202 vs 303,404) - 4 distinct capabilities, not 5. The §15 denominator was inflated, making the vital-sign ratio look ~30% healthier than true
paid: 2026-07-04, every vitals sample since founding recorded a too-low ratio; corrected turn 46 (count drops 5->4, ratio 4.4M->6.3M)
guard: counts entering a metric dedupe by identity, never array length; refute.md caught it - the pass that asked 'duplicates or distinct?' found they were distinct re-certs

what: capstone kill-condition audit fanned 46 Opus auditors + refuters in parallel; burned ~2.06M tokens / 47 agents / 320 tool-uses to check 23 falsifiers (rest died on session cap). Each falsifier was decidable by 1-3 grep/git commands — a cheap-model or inline pass would have cost ~2 orders of magnitude less.
paid: 2026-07-04, session usage 30%->100% in ~90s; audit left 23/46 organs unchecked
guard: kill-condition checks are grep-shaped, not reasoning-shaped: run them inline or via station llm (free local), NOT one Opus agent per check. Reserve Opus fan-out for genuinely irreducible judgment. A cheap check dispatched expensively is the exact token-doctrine violation the architecture exists to prevent.
